title: E2E Human-Friendly Feedback System — Message constants, Toast layer, mmff-cli, Hints
scope: Four work streams that replace machine-oriented API error strings with human-readable copy, add a global toast notification layer to the frontend, introduce a compiled Go CLI for authenticated API access, and lay the groundwork for a contextual hints system.
value: Today users see raw backend strings like "invalid credentials", "invalid id", and "not found" directly in the UI. There is no global success or error notification channel — all feedback is scattered across 15+ local useState instances. Success states are completely silent. This plan closes the full feedback loop: every error becomes readable copy, every mutation gets an acknowledgement, and developers get a power-user CLI to interact with the API without curl boilerplate.
implementation_plan: ✓ WS1-A: Create backend/internal/messages/messages.go with all human-readable constants; update auth/handler.go, security/csrf.go, auth/middleware.go to use them and migrate http.Error calls to httperr.Write (commit 633366e)
✓ WS2-A: Install Sonner, create app/components/Toaster.tsx + app/lib/toast.ts, mount <Toaster /> in app/layout.tsx (commit 633366e)

⧰ WS2-B: Migrate per-component error useState → notify.apiError(); add success toasts to key mutations (AuthContext, ProfileBar, ArchiveMapFlyout done; need library-releases, preferences/navigation, other handlers)
WS4-A: Scaffold backend/cmd/cli/ with Cobra root + auth subcommands (login, logout, me) + shared HTTP client + session store (~/.mmff/session.json)
WS1-B: Batch-update remaining ~400 httperr.Write call sites across all handlers to reference messages.* constants
WS4-B: Expand mmff-cli to cover all major resource groups (portfolio-models, portfolio-items, roles, work-items, sprints)
WS3: Create app/lib/hints.ts constants; wire notify.hint() to first-interaction triggers with localStorage suppression
areas_impacted: Backend: backend/internal/messages/ (new), all handler files (~408 httperr.Write sites), security/csrf.go, auth/middleware.go
Frontend: app/layout.tsx, app/components/Toaster.tsx (new), app/lib/toast.ts (new), app/lib/hints.ts (new), ~15 component files
Docs: api-reference/ — update error shape docs to reflect human-readable detail strings
feature_list: Consistent RFC 9457 error shape across all endpoints including middleware
Global Sonner toast layer (success, error, info, hint variants)
notify.apiError() helper maps ApiError → toast automatically
mmff-cli compiled binary: auth login/logout/me, resource list/get/create, --json flag, shell completion
work_item_backlog: WS1-B: Batch handler message constants rollout
acceptance_criteria: Login with bad credentials → detail reads 'The email or password you entered is incorrect.'
Any API error triggered from the UI → Sonner toast appears bottom-right with human-readable copy
mmff-cli auth login --email user@mmffdev.com stores token and prints user summary
mmff-cli portfolio-models list returns formatted table
go build -o server ./cmd/server completes with zero errors
risks: RISK-LOW: ~408 httperr.Write sites in WS1-B — mechanical find-replace but large surface; batch carefully and verify build after each package
RISK-LOW: Sonner SSR — Next.js App Router requires 'use client' wrapper; already handled by Toaster.tsx wrapper component
RISK-LOW: mmff-cli Cobra dependency adds to go.mod — isolated to cmd/cli, no impact on server binary
